← About ODIPA
Press & Media

Media Resources

ODIPA's experts are available for interviews, commentary, and background briefings on digital privacy, data protection policy, and consumer rights.

Contact Press Team
Press inquiriespress@odipa.org
Response timeSame business day
AvailabilityPhone, video, on-record, background
IncorporatedNovember 14, 2024 — California
Nonprofit status501(c)(3) — EIN: 33-2725121
Areas of Expert Commentary

ODIPA staff and advisors are available to provide independent, nonpartisan expert commentary across a wide range of privacy topics.

Privacy Legislation

State and federal privacy bills, regulatory rulemaking, and enforcement actions by the FTC and state attorneys general.

Data Breaches

Consumer impact analysis, breach notification requirements, and guidance on protective steps for affected individuals.

Platform Privacy

Social media data practices, ad targeting, terms of service analysis, and consumer rights vis-à-vis tech platforms.

Vulnerable Populations

Privacy issues disproportionately affecting seniors, children, and low-income communities — and the policies that address them.

Corporate Compliance

How businesses are (and aren't) complying with CCPA/CPRA, GDPR, GLBA, BSA, PCI DSS, HIPAA, HITECH, NERC CIP, BIPA, FERPA, COPPA, SOC 2, NIST, and the growing patchwork of state privacy laws.

AI & Data Privacy

The intersection of artificial intelligence, machine learning, and consumer privacy rights in an evolving regulatory landscape.

Press Releases
January 2026

ODIPA Receives IRS 501(c)(3) Tax-Exempt Status

ODIPA announced receipt of its IRS determination letter granting 501(c)(3) tax-exempt status, confirming the organization's standing as a public charity and enabling tax-deductible contributions.

Request full release →
2026

ODIPA Launches odipa.org and Eight Public Programs

ODIPA released odipa.org as a fully open-source Next.js website and launched eight consumer education, advocacy, and technical programs as part of its public debut.

Request full release →
November 2024

ODIPA Incorporated in California

ODIPA — Organization for Digital Information Privacy & Awareness — was incorporated in California on November 14, 2024, establishing its legal structure and charitable purpose.

Request full release →
Expert Interview Topics
✓CCPA/CPRA consumer rights and business compliance
✓Financial services privacy — GLBA, BSA, PCI DSS, and data sharing
✓Healthcare data privacy — HIPAA, HITECH, and emerging threats
✓Federal privacy legislation landscape
✓Data broker industry and opt-out strategies
✓Privacy implications of AI and machine learning
✓Senior and vulnerable population privacy risks
✓Children's online privacy (COPPA and beyond)
✓Corporate data practices and certification standards
✓International privacy law comparisons (GDPR vs US)
Media Guidelines

How should ODIPA be identified in coverage?

ODIPA (Organization for Digital Information Privacy & Awareness) is a 501(c)(3) nonprofit organization based in Los Angeles, California, focused on consumer digital privacy education and advocacy.

Is ODIPA available for on-the-record quotes?

Yes. ODIPA spokespeople are available for on-the-record commentary, background briefings, and attributed expert analysis. We are nonpartisan and do not make political endorsements.

Can I use ODIPA's logo and materials?

Press and media may use ODIPA's logo and research with proper attribution. High-resolution assets are available by request at press@odipa.org.

What is your embargo policy?

ODIPA will honor reasonable embargo requests from media partners for joint announcements. Contact press@odipa.org to discuss.

Press Contact

For media inquiries, interview requests, and press materials, contact our communications team directly.

press@odipa.org

Same-day response during business hours

Also Available
Media KitRequest via email
Hi-res Logopress@odipa.org
Fact SheetRequest via email
Annual ReportView →
Organization for Digital Information Privacy & Awareness

Educating consumers and organizations about digital information privacy — advocating for practices and policies that protect people in an increasingly data-driven world.

ODIPA is funded through individual donations, corporate sponsorships, and foundation grants.

Privacy Newsletter

Privacy digest: breach alerts, new laws, research releases.

No spam, ever. Unsubscribe any time. We never sell your data. Privacy Policy
